consequence_relation.name: Hohfeldian correlative and opposition relation layer
consequence_relation.notes: The first slice represents Hohfeldian positions as directed relations between two agents and an act, with correlative and opposite-position laws kept explicit.

metalogical_properties.description: Every Hohfeldian position is represented as an Agent-Agent-Act relation.
Claim-right/Duty, Privilege/No-claim, Power/Liability, and Immunity/Disability are represented as explicit equivalence laws.
Claim-right/No-claim, Privilege/Duty, Power/Disability, and Immunity/Liability are represented as explicit consistency constraints.
The modal consequence analysis from the paper is intentionally left for a later slice after this relational kernel is imported into a modal semantics.

normative_systems.description: A holder's claim-right toward a bearer is correlated with the bearer's directed duty toward the holder.
Higher-order legal change positions are represented by the Power/Liability and Immunity/Disability correlative pairs.
A small Lean example checks a directed claim-right, duty, power, and liability relation.

permission_families.description: Privilege is represented as a directed Hohfeldian permission-like position whose correlative is No-claim.

formalization_targets.reason: Represented by HohfeldFrame as Agent-Agent-Act relations.
Represented by CorrelativeLaws and exported Lean theorems.
Represented by OppositionLaws and exported Lean theorems.
The first slice records the relational core; full conditional/modal consequence semantics should be added in a follow-up profile.
